dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, we lost people in the last attack. Good people. And the ones who survived are falling apart.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} raid took lives: a guard, a worker, an elder who refused to abandon their post. We drove them off, but the victory feels hollow. {target_npc} hasn't spoken since the burial. Half the settlement is talking about abandoning this place. I need someone to gather {quest_item} from {location_hint} so we can build a proper remembrance.
dialogueChunks.outro: The {enemy_type} are still prowling the roads, but if we don't give people something to rally around, grief will destroy what the raid couldn't. {quest_stakes} depends on it. {quest_reward_hint} is the shared strength we need.

playerResponses.text: Honoring the dead gives the living a reason to keep going. I'll help.
Memorial materials through hostile territory. It's somber work, but I'll do it.
Grief counseling isn't in my skill set. I'm sorry for your loss.

dialogueChunks.intro: My partner was killed on the road two weeks ago, {player_name}. I've made my peace with that. But there's one thing I can't let go of.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They were carrying {quest_item} when the {enemy_type} ambushed the caravan {location_hint}. It was a gift they'd made for our child: the only thing our child would have to remember them by. The caravan was looted and the spoils were carried off. {target_npc} says the {enemy_type} probably took it to their camp.
dialogueChunks.outro: I'm not asking you to avenge them. I'm asking you to {quest_action} and bring home the one thing my child will hold when asking about their parent. {quest_stakes} is everything to us. {quest_threat} is the finality of it.

playerResponses.text: A parent's last gift to their child. I'll find it, no matter what's in the way.
Raiding an enemy camp for a keepsake. It's personal, but I'll do it for a price.
Charging into an enemy camp for a single item is too dangerous.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I should have died instead. I know that's not a productive thought, but it's the only one I have most days.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My partner and I were building something together at the edge of the settlement. They were gathering {quest_item} from {location_hint} when the {enemy_type} found them. I was supposed to go that day, but I stayed behind because I was tired. Tired. That's the reason I'm alive and they're not.
dialogueChunks.outro: {target_npc} has been trying to get me to function. But the only thing that helps is the thought of finishing what we started. I need the materials they were gathering. I need to {quest_action}. {quest_stakes} gives me a reason to keep going. {quest_reward_hint} would be a purpose renewed.

playerResponses.text: Building something in their memory is a way to carry them forward. I'll get the materials.
Survivor's guilt is a heavy burden. I'll help lighten it however I can.
I'm sorry for your loss, but I can't fight your grief for you.
